channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan Reaffirms Strong Support to Saudi Arabia Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: On Wednesday, Prime Minister’s spokesperson for foreign media, Mosharraf Zaidi, clarified Pakistan’s stance regarding the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. He told Bloomberg TV that Pakistan would ...

item.title: Nunavut MP Lori Idlout joins Liberal caucus, strengthening Carney's position
item.description: Nunavut Member of Parliament Lori Idlout has officially crossed the floor from the NDP to join Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal government. Idlout, first elected in 2021, becomes the fourth MP in ...

item.title: Hellenic Club appeals $1.2m fine over alleged failures to address gambling harm
item.description: The Hellenic Club in Canberra is challenging a $1.2 million fine imposed after the tragic death of Raimo 'Ray' Kasurinen, who took his own life in March 2021 following severe gambling losses. At the A...

item.title: Zach Top on winning his first Grammy and keeping humour in country music
item.description: Zach Top, one of the brightest rising stars in country music, has been making waves since his debut, but the 28-year-old remains refreshingly grounded even after winning his first Grammy Award for Bes...

item.title: Tanzanians stranded in Dubai due to Middle East conflict return safely home
item.description: Emotional scenes were seen at Julius Nyerere International Airport (JNIA) on Monday evening as 236 Tanzanians who had been stranded in Dubai due to escalating tensions in the Middle East returned home...

item.title: Report Highlights Financial Struggles Among Canadians Over 50
item.description: A recent report has revealed that one in five Canadians over the age of 50 is living at a standard of living considered equivalent to the poverty line. The study highlights ongoing financial challenge...

item.title: Over 50 Immigration Officers Dismissed as BMA Intensifies Anti-Corruption Measures
item.description: The Border Management Authority (BMA) has terminated the employment of over 50 immigration officials involved in corrupt practices at key ports of entry, including Beitbridge, Lebombo, Oshoek, Maseru ...

item.title: Palace strengthens international collaboration to advance BARMM peace process
item.description: Malacañang has stepped up efforts to deepen international partnerships in support of the Bangsamoro peace process. The Office of the Special Assistant to the President (OSAP), through the Inter-Cabine...

item.title: Understanding Tubal Ligation and Its Long-Term Implications
item.description: Tubal ligation, popularly known as 'tying your tubes,' is becoming more common among Kenyan women seeking permanent contraception. Dr Felix Oindi, an obstetrician-gynaecologist at Aga Khan University ...

item.title: Designing Inclusive Driverless Vehicles for People with Hearing Loss
item.description: Self-driving cars are becoming a reality in the UK, with automated vehicles already being tested on public roads and self-driving taxi services anticipated in 2026. The upcoming Automated Vehicles Act...

item.title: Bishop Subpoenaed by NBI for Witnessing Ex-Marines’ Affidavit
item.description: The National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) has issued a subpoena to Bishop Efraim Tendero, who served as a witness to the affidavit signed by 18 alleged former Philippine Marines. NBI Director Melvin ...

item.title: Nigerian Midfielder Daniel Daga Sentenced to Six Months in Prison by Norwegian Court for Sexual Act Without Consent, Plans to Appeal
item.description: Nigerian footballer Daniel Daga, the 19-year-old midfielder wey dey play for Molde FK in Norway, don land six months prison sentence after Norwegian court find am guilty of committing sexual act witho...

item.title: Pentagon War Briefings Include Public Prayer and High Food Costs
item.description: In a recent episode of his podcast, Pete Hegseth discusses a day of briefings from the White House and the Pentagon concerning potential conflict with Iran. The episode highlights a combination of ser...

item.title: Australia’s Migration Policies: Humanitarian Support and Temporary Visa Restrictions
item.description: Over the past 48 hours, Australia’s migration landscape has highlighted both the government’s humanitarian efforts and its strict border policies. The federal government granted refugee status to memb...

item.title: Historic Cape Dutch Farmhouse Restored as Community Hub in Cape Town
item.description: In Cape Town’s Glencairn Valley, the 19th-century Cape Dutch homestead known as Welcome Cottage has been revitalised into a lively community centre through a collaborative effort between the South Afr...

item.title: Fuel Shortage Causes Delays for Tanker Drivers Across Pakistan
item.description: Tanker drivers across Pakistan have reported significant delays at fuel depots due to a shortage of petrol, as the ongoing Middle East conflict between the US, Israel, and Iran disrupts shipping and d...

item.title: High Court Rules in Favor of Australian Designer Katie Perry in Trademark Dispute with Singer Katy Perry
item.description: Australian designer Katie Perry, who legally changed her surname to Taylor in 2015, has won a landmark High Court appeal in a long-standing trademark dispute with US pop star Katy Perry. The case revo...

item.title: Exim Bank Tanzania Honoured with Two Mastercard Awards for 2025
item.description: Exim Bank Tanzania has been recognised with two big Mastercard awards for 2025, showing strong financial growth and top-notch operations. The bank won for Best Elite Banking Proposition and Highest Ca...

item.title: Google Expands Gemini AI Integration in Chrome to India, Canada, and New Zealand
item.description: Google has expanded its Gemini AI integration in Chrome to new regions, including India, Canada, and New Zealand. The feature appears as a sidebar on desktop, allowing users to interact with Google’s ...

item.title: Escalating Iran-Israel Conflict: Saudi Missile Interceptions and Israeli Strikes on Beirut Reported
item.description: The Middle East conflict involving Iran, Israel, and the United States has intensified significantly. On March 11, 2026, Saudi Arabia reported intercepting seven ballistic missiles targeting an air ba...
